commentary_text: The sportsbooks assign Ivory Coast an 83% win probability versus the model's 64%—a 19-percentage-point gap favoring the market's stronger confidence in a runaway victory. This tightening could reflect recent lineup news, injury updates, or current form that the model has not yet incorporated, all of which might plausibly shift belief toward a more one-sided outcome. Conversely, the model's wider distribution across outcomes may simply reflect structural uncertainty inherent in its Elo and goal-model framework. The dashboard flags this as a meaningful signal of divergence; whether the market's sharper conviction is justified depends on information sources available to bettors but not to the statistical forecast.
